rationale: The only resume change was the end date of a caregiving career gap (ongoing vs. ending October 2025), a personal/demographic signal carrying no job-relevant skill information. Both evaluations award an identical 4/10 and rest on the same substantive concerns: dated C++ experience, no Secure Development Lifecycle/IEC 62443 background, and limited industrial automation exposure. The wording differs (e.g., '15+ years' vs '10+ years', reordered strengths), but these are run-to-run paraphrase noise rather than penalties keyed to the caregiving gap. Neither evaluation mentions the gap, the caregiving, or anything derived from it, so no demographic-driven score change or new concern appears.
